# MLX-Gen Full Documentation Context

> MLX-Gen is a local MLX image and video generation runtime for Apple Silicon. It is published as
> the `mlx-gen` Python package and exposes the `mlxgen` command surface for generation, model
> download, model preparation, capability inspection, and application integration.

## Document Index

- `README.md`: project overview, install commands, mflux credit, main workflows, published model links, Wan TI2V/A14B notes, and ecosystem links.
- `docs/README.md`: documentation tree index.
- `docs/getting-started.md`: install, first download, model preparation, image generation, image editing, and Wan video examples.
- `docs/api.md`: public CLI, router behavior, task/mode resolution, I2I mode selection, outpaint/reframe status, Wan video sizes, `mlxgen capabilities`, and Python routing helpers.
- `docs/examples/spaceship-snow.md`: complete model-backed workflow across T2I, I2I edit, multi-reference I2I, T2V A14B, and I2V A14B.
- `docs/model-management.md`: cache-only runtime policy, explicit `download` and `prepare`, local model folders, and `DownloadRequiredError`.
- `docs/quantization.md`: complete published package matrix, q8/q4/BF16 policies, model-family compatibility, and Wan TI2V/A14B package status.
- `docs/huggingface-publishing.md`: generated model cards and AbstractFramework Hugging Face upload guidance.
- `docs/python-integration.md`: embedding MLX-Gen, capability checks, progress callbacks, AbstractVision integration, and threading behavior.
- `docs/faq.md`: common conceptual questions, I2I mode selection, outpaint/reframe status, Wan resolutions, prompting guidance, and usage limits.
- `docs/troubleshooting.md`: user-facing failure symptoms, diagnostics, and fixes.
- `docs/release.md`: release and publishing process.
- `ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS.md`: upstream mflux credit and model/community acknowledgements.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md`: local development and pull-request expectations.
- `SECURITY.md`: vulnerability reporting and safety guidance.
- `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`: participation expectations.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: user-visible release history.

---

## Project Summary

MLX-Gen runs supported image and video generation models locally with MLX. It started as a fork of
`filipstrand/mflux`, and most credit for the current codebase remains with Filip Strand and the
original mflux contributors. MLX-Gen now publishes independently as `mlx-gen` and exposes `mlxgen`
as the public command surface.

The project focuses on practical Apple Silicon workflows:

- cache-only generation after explicit setup;
- prepared local model folders, including quantized layouts;
- Qwen Image/Edit, FLUX.2 Klein, Z-Image, ERNIE Image Turbo, Bonsai Image, FIBO, and Wan2.2 support;
- model capability inspection before heavy runs;
- progress events for applications;
- integration with AbstractVision, AbstractFramework, and AbstractFlow.

Some implementation internals still use the original `mflux.*` package layout. New user-facing
commands and integrations should prefer `mlxgen`.

---

## Runtime Contract

MLX-Gen separates setup from generation:

- `mlxgen download` explicitly downloads model artifacts into the local cache.
- `mlxgen prepare` explicitly creates reusable local MLX-Gen model folders and generated model cards.
- `mlxgen generate` runs from cached or prepared files and does not start network downloads.
- `mlxgen capabilities` inspects public tasks, internal modes, and option support without loading weights.

Missing model, tokenizer, LoRA, or Depth Pro artifacts raise `DownloadRequiredError` with explicit
download or prepare commands. This behavior is important for application integrations because long
jobs should not unexpectedly start a network transfer.

---

## Public Tasks And Internal Modes

Public tasks describe media direction:

- `text-to-image`
- `image-to-image`
- `text-to-video`
- `image-to-video`

Image editing is not a separate public task. It is an internal image-to-image mode. The resolver
uses model capabilities, image count, and options to choose a generation plan:

- no image: text-to-image or text-to-video when the model supports it;
- one image and video model: image-to-video;
- one image plus `--image-strength`: latent img2img;
- one image with FLUX.2 or a dedicated edit checkpoint and no `--image-strength`: edit/reference I2I;
- one image with a latent image model such as ERNIE Image Turbo, Z-Image, or a base Qwen/FIBO generation variant: latent img2img unless a supported edit route is explicitly requested;
- repeated `--image`: multi-reference I2I when supported;
- mask/outpaint options: accepted only by a capability that supports them.

Applications can use `get_model_capabilities(...)` and `resolve_generation_plan(...)` from the
`mlxgen` package to make the same decision before launching a job.

`--image-strength` belongs only to latent img2img. Edit/reference and multi-reference I2I use
source images as conditioning or references, so MLX-Gen rejects `--image-strength` for those modes
before loading weights. Reframing/outpainting is not first-class in unified `mlxgen generate` yet;
generic I2I resizing is not a substitute for masked outpainting because it does not preserve the
original pixels in place.

---

## Reproducible Example

`docs/examples/spaceship-snow.md` is the primary complete example. It downloads:

- `AbstractFramework/flux.2-klein-9b-8bit`
- `AbstractFramework/wan2.2-t2v-a14b-diffusers-8bit`
- `AbstractFramework/wan2.2-i2v-a14b-diffusers-8bit`

It then generates:

- a text-to-image spaceship in snow;
- a single-image pencil-sketch edit;
- a single-image crash-in-snow edit;
- a multi-reference image composition using the sketch style and crash layout;
- a Wan A14B text-to-video takeoff clip;
- a Wan A14B image-to-video takeoff clip from the generated source image.

The included docs assets contain the generated images, video frame strips, MP4 files, and a contact
sheet assembled from real outputs.

---

## Wan Video Sizes

Wan frame counts are normalized to `4n + 1`, and duration is `frames / fps`. Width and height are
normalized to the selected model's VAE/patch multiple:

- TI2V-5B T2V/I2V requires 32-pixel multiples. Recommended/native quality sizes are `1280x704` and
  `704x1280`; practical lower-cost sizes include `832x480`, `480x832`, `448x256`, and `256x448`.
  `1280x720` adjusts to `1280x704`, and `432x240` adjusts to `416x224`.
- T2V-A14B and I2V-A14B require 16-pixel multiples. Recommended/native quality sizes are
  `1280x720` and `720x1280`; practical lower-cost sizes include `832x480`, `480x832`, `448x256`,
  `256x448`, and `432x240`.

Lower-cost sizes are useful for routing checks and prompt iteration. Use the recommended/native
size, frame count, and step count when judging visual quality.

---

## Published Models

Prepared MLX-Gen model folders are published under the Hugging Face
`AbstractFramework` organization. Representative current repositories include FLUX.2 Klein 4B/9B
q4/q8 packages, Qwen Image and Qwen Image Edit q4/q8 packages, Z-Image and Z-Image Turbo q4/q8
packages, ERNIE Image Turbo q8, a nominal ERNIE `-4bit` repo that currently has q8 metadata,
Wan2.2 TI2V-5B q8, and Wan2.2 A14B T2V/I2V BF16 and mixed q8/BF16 packages. There is no published
TI2V-5B BF16 or q4/mixed q4/q8 package yet.

Prepared folders are MLX-Gen/mflux saved-weight layouts. They are intended for MLX-Gen and
compatible mflux code, not direct Diffusers or Transformers `from_pretrained()` loading.

---

## Quantization Summary

MLX-Gen treats low-bit model preparation as model-specific. Qwen and ERNIE use mixed q4/q8 policies
because fully q4 layouts can visibly degrade generation. Bonsai uses Prism's pre-packed ternary
2-bit checkpoint rather than MLX-Gen `prepare`.

Wan A14B uses a mixed q8/BF16 policy for 8-bit packages:

- q8 for bulky transformer block linears;
- BF16 for the Wan VAE;
- BF16 for sensitive conditioning/output projection paths;
- BF16 for the UMT5 text encoder, tokenizer files, scheduler metadata, norms, convolutions, and
  non-quantizable parameters.

For TI2V-5B, the upstream source snapshot is about 31.9 GiB and stores transformer and VAE files as
FP32 while the UMT5 text encoder is BF16. MLX-Gen loads and prepares Wan transformer/VAE weights at
BF16 runtime precision. The current q8 package is about 16.9 GiB; a future prepared BF16 package
would mainly be a smaller source-equivalent folder, not a separate runtime-memory optimization.

On an Apple M5 Max with 128 GB unified memory, published-card validation measured:

- T2V-A14B BF16: 64.3 GiB disk, 33.0 GiB physical peak, 31.8 GiB max RSS, 27.7 GiB MLX peak,
  152.7 s on 384x224, 33 frames, 12 steps, 8 fps.
- T2V-A14B mixed q8/BF16: 39.7 GiB disk, 20.7 GiB physical peak, 19.5 GiB max RSS, 15.5 GiB MLX
  peak, 154.8 s on the same profile.
- I2V-A14B BF16: 64.1 GiB disk, 33.7 GiB physical peak, 31.8 GiB max RSS, 28.2 GiB MLX peak,
  228.2 s on 384x384, 33 frames, 12 steps, 8 fps.
- I2V-A14B mixed q8/BF16: 39.7 GiB disk, 21.5 GiB physical peak, 19.6 GiB max RSS, 15.9 GiB MLX
  peak, 242.2 s on the same profile.

In those validation runs, mixed q8/BF16 reduced disk usage by about 38% versus prepared BF16 folders
and reduced full-process physical peak memory by about 36-37%. It is not documented as a speed
improvement.

---

## Ecosystem

MLX-Gen is used by:

- AbstractVision: the vision and generation layer for local/cross-platform workflows;
- AbstractFramework: the broader framework for agentic and generative workflows;
- AbstractFlow: visual orchestration that can compose generative capabilities with persistent
  agentic tasks.

MLX-Gen owns the local MLX runtime boundary: model loading, capability resolution, quantized local
formats, fail-fast missing-artifact behavior, and progress events.

---

## Safety And Contribution Notes

Model weights remain governed by their source licenses and access requirements. Hugging Face tokens
and private paths should not be committed. Security issues should be reported according to
`SECURITY.md`.

Contributors should keep user-facing docs aligned with runnable commands, avoid documenting
unsupported routes, and follow the model-backed validation policy in the ADRs when adding new model
support.
